tasks.title: Caching Key Normalization PoC
tasks.description: Develop a proof of concept for normalizing natural language queries to maximize cache hit rates. The initial PoC has been completed, with future validation planned using real traffic logs (Phase 2C).
tasks.details: Implemented an algorithm to strip stop words and fillers (e.g., '혹시', '한번'), unify terminal endings, normalize spacing and punctuation, and hash the intent using SHA-256. Defined context wrappers via the `CacheKeyContext` class to ensure accurate cache retrieval for semantically identical questions. Key components include `build_cache_key` and `ttl_for` functions implemented within `src/cache/key_normalizer.py`.
tasks.testStrategy: Validated through 15 unit tests in `src/cache/tests/test_key_normalizer.py`. Specifically verified that 5 variations of interrogative queries successfully resolve to the exact same cache key with zero false positives. Future testing will involve real traffic logs to measure overall cache hit rates post-Phase 2C.

subtasks.testStrategy: Validated that identical normalized queries with different contexts result in distinct cache keys, preventing cross-context false positives.

subtasks.title: Generate SHA-256 Cache Key
subtasks.description: Hash the combined normalized string and context wrappers using SHA-256 to generate the final cache key.
subtasks.details: Implemented the `build_cache_key` and `ttl_for` functions in `src/cache/key_normalizer.py`. `build_cache_key` deterministically hashes the query and `CacheKeyContext` utilizing SHA-256.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verified that 5 variations of identical intents successfully run through the pipeline and generate the exact same SHA-256 hash.

subtasks.testStrategy: Seed the database with mock user activity and payment data, then verify the admin dashboard accurately calculates and displays MRR, MAU, Churn, and operational costs.

tasks.title: Final Release Checks & Production Deployment
tasks.description: Execute the production deployment runbook and switch DNS to live.
tasks.details: Deploy final Docker images. Execute zero-downtime database migrations. Ensure backup systems (daily pg_dump to S3) are active. Monitor logs meticulously for the first 24 hours.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify the live environment passes a smoke test suite. Run a database recovery drill from S3 to ensure RPO < 24h targets are met.

subtasks.title: Pre-Deployment Backup and Environment Validation
subtasks.description: Validate the production environment configurations and ensure daily S3 pg_dump backup systems are fully active.
subtasks.details: Verify all production environment variables and secrets. Trigger a manual pre-deployment database backup via pg_dump to S3 and verify the archive's integrity to guarantee safe recovery capability.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run a database recovery drill from the generated S3 snapshot to ensure RPO < 24h targets are met and data is intact.

subtasks.title: Execute Zero-Downtime Database Migrations
subtasks.description: Apply database schema changes to the production database utilizing zero-downtime deployment patterns.
subtasks.details: Execute additive schema migrations first (e.g., adding tables/columns without dropping old ones). Ensure the current application versions remain fully operational during the schema transition.
subtasks.testStrategy: Monitor database connection pools and execution times during the migration. Verify legacy API endpoints continue to return 200 OK without errors.

subtasks.title: Deploy Final Docker Images and Execute DNS Switch
subtasks.description: Roll out the final production Docker images via rolling updates and orchestrate the DNS switch to the live environment.
subtasks.details: Deploy the final Docker images to the production orchestrator using a rolling update strategy to prevent downtime. Once health checks pass on the new containers, update DNS A/CNAME records to route traffic to the live load balancer.
subtasks.testStrategy: Use global DNS propagation tracking tools to confirm the switch. Perform immediate health-check pings on the live domain.

subtasks.title: Post-Deployment Monitoring and Smoke Testing
subtasks.description: Conduct live environment smoke tests and meticulously monitor production application and database logs for 24 hours.
subtasks.details: Run the complete post-deployment runbook. Establish a monitoring watch for the first 24 hours, focusing on unexpected application errors, database lock contention, and zero-downtime migration side-effects.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the live environment passes a comprehensive smoke test suite simulating critical user paths. Review error tracking systems (e.g., Sentry) for unhandled exceptions.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Create a step-by-step production runbook including the zero-downtime database migration process.

tasks.title: Phase 3 Beta Closing & KPI Redefinition
tasks.description: Analyze external beta metrics to redefine the 120 paid customers KPI and update the final business report for the regional newspaper committee.
tasks.details: Extract analytics data from the 100 external beta users, specifically focusing on: (1) Free-to-premium actual conversion rates, (2) Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) separated by B2B and B2C segments, and (3) Churn cohort trends. Conduct a review session with the Project Manager and Digital Transformation Lead before 2026-11-30. Based on the data, formally redefine the initial 120 paid customers KPI into one of three models: (a) MRR single KPI, (b) Weighted KPI (B2B=5 points, B2C=1 point), or (c) Maintain the original business plan. Document the empirical rationale and finalize the Business Result Report required for the regional newspaper development committee.

tasks.title: Prepare Citizen Journalist Recruitment Materials (v1.5 Schedule Update)
tasks.description: Draft recruitment notices, application forms, evaluation criteria, interview questions, and a 6-session training curriculum for the rescheduled mid-July citizen journalist program.
tasks.details: This task focuses on the preparatory work required for the citizen journalist recruitment, which has been delayed from May to mid-July 2026 to ensure the platform is sufficiently functional for new recruits. Deliverables must be completed by 2026-06-30. 1. Recruitment Notice: Write promotional copy, outline eligibility requirements, and clearly state the updated timeline. 2. Application Form: Create a structured Google Form (or custom platform form) to capture applicant details. 3. Evaluation Criteria: Develop a comprehensive scoring matrix that ensures regional balance across Eup and Myeon districts, age diversity, reporting motivation, and baseline literacy/writing skills. 4. Interview Questionnaire: Prepare standardized behavioral and scenario-based interview questions. 5. Training Curriculum: Draft an outline for a 6-session educational program covering journalism ethics, basic reporting, and practical usage of the new publishing platform.
tasks.testStrategy: Conduct a stakeholder review session with the editorial team and project manager to approve all 5 document deliverables. Perform a dry-run submission of the Google Form/application form to ensure data fields are accurately captured and easily exportable for the evaluation matrix. Confirm that the finalized schedule is updated in the master project roadmap.

tasks.title: Execute Citizen Journalist Recruitment and Selection
tasks.description: Conduct a two-week application and one-week interview process to select 12 diverse citizen journalists for a shortened three-month program.
tasks.details: Execute the recruitment campaign scheduled from 2026-07-15 to 2026-07-31. Step 1: Publish recruitment notices on the main taeannews.co.kr website, the new insight platform, and official SNS channels using materials prepared in Task #42. Step 2: Manage the 2-week application intake phase, actively monitoring submission rates. Step 3: Conduct a 1-week interview and portfolio evaluation phase. The AI Co-Pilot Web Editor (Task #25) must be showcased in its beta state during interviews to assess candidates' adaptability to AI tools. Step 4: Finalize the selection of exactly 12 journalists. The final cohort must guarantee regional balance (distributing slots across Anmyeon, Sowon, Geunheung, and Taean eup/myeon) and age diversity (spanning 20s, 30s, 40s, 50s, and 60s). Crucially, because the activity period has been shortened to an intensive 3-month window (September to November), interviewers must explicitly verify and document each candidate's ability to successfully balance the reporting workload with their primary occupation.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that recruitment posts are live across all three channels (taeannews.co.kr, Insight, SNS) by 2026-07-15. Audit the final selection roster to confirm it contains exactly 12 individuals, passes the regional distribution check (at least one from Anmyeon, Sowon, Geunheung, and Taean), and passes the age diversity check (at least one representative from each decade 20s-60s). Review the interview evaluation matrices to ensure the 'main job parallel capacity' criterion was scored for all selected candidates.
